name: NSA Naples
tagline: The Navy's hub in the Mediterranean, supporting the U.S. Sixth Fleet from the Bay of Naples in southern Italy.
_note: True OCONUS (Italy). OHA housing, DoDEA Europe schools, command-sponsorship/SOFA notes, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy sections (US-only feeds). Naval Support Activity Naples supports U.S. Sixth Fleet / Navy in the Med. Two sites: Capodichino (near the airport, HQ) and the Support Site at Gricignano (housing, commissary, hospital, DoDEA schools). Medical: U.S. Naval Hospital Naples (Support Site). Facts verified from official sources (cnic.navy.mil NSA Naples, USNH Naples, DeCA/NEX, DoDEA Europe, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Role: The Navy's primary hub in the Mediterranean
data.Supports: U.S. Sixth Fleet and U.S. naval forces across the Med
data.Two sites: Capodichino (HQ, near the airport) and the Support Site at Gricignano (housing, schools, hospital)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Support Activity Naples is the Navy's primary hub in the Mediterranean, supporting the U.S. Sixth Fleet and American naval forces across the region. It operates from two main sites: Capodichino, the headquarters area near the Naples airport, and the Support Site at Gricignano, which holds much of the housing, the commissary, the hospital, and the schools.
Set on the Bay of Naples in southern Italy, it's a vibrant, chaotic, and beautiful posting, with Rome, the Amalfi Coast, and the rest of Europe within reach.

sections.title: An Italy Tour: Support Site vs. Living on the Economy

data.subtitle: Naples is a beloved but intense overseas tour. The main choice is housing at the Support Site versus renting 'on the economy' in the Italian community, each with real trade-offs.
intro: Most families come command-sponsored and choose between government housing (much of it at the Support Site in Gricignano) and renting on the Italian economy with the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA).

The Support Site is the simplest, most American option with schools and services on site. Living on the economy means a richer Italian experience and often more space, balanced against an Italian lease, the language, and Naples-area traffic.

recommendations.advice: Government housing at the Support Site (Gricignano) puts you near schools, the commissary, and the hospital, ideal for a first overseas tour.
Renting on the economy in the surrounding towns offers the real Italian experience, with OHA to offset rent.
The housing office assigns government housing and must approve economy rentals, and helps with leases, utilities, and OHA, so start there before signing anything.
Rome, Pompeii, the Amalfi Coast, and the rest of Europe are within reach; lean into the travel.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access follows U.S. and host-nation (SOFA) rules. Visitors without a DoD ID need to be sponsored and registered through the Visitor Control Center.
Requirements for overseas installations can be stricter and change with force-protection levels, so confirm the current details on the official NSA Naples site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (Government & OHA)
data: Much of the government family housing is at the Support Site in Gricignano, and many families instead live on the Italian economy in the surrounding towns, receiving the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns government housing and must approve economy rentals; it also helps with leases, utilities, and OHA paperwork. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of U.S. personnel attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools at the Support Site (Gricignano), serving students from elementary through high school.
Confirm current schools, boundaries, and registration through DoDEA Europe and the command, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Hospital: U.S. Naval Hospital Naples (Support Site, Gricignano)
data.Care: A military hospital with primary and specialty outpatient care and some inpatient services; complex care via host-nation and regional referral

intro: An overseas PCS to Italy has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Get these sorted early to settle in smoothly.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: Make sure your family is command-sponsored before or upon arrival; it drives your OHA, DoDEA enrollment, and travel entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, update DEERS, and get overseas ID and logistics cards as required.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose Support Site housing or get approval and help to rent on the economy with OHA; claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) while you look.

steps.title: Handle SOFA, license, and vehicle
steps.text: Get your SOFA driver's license, register or ship a vehicle, and complete SOFA documentation. Confirm whether family members need a no-fee (official) passport.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Naples schools (Support Site) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program so you can be seen at U.S. Naval Hospital Naples and host-nation providers.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Naples?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for economy rentals, or you live in government housing. OHA is based on your actual rent up to a cap, not the stateside BAH tables.
Either in government housing (much of it at the Support Site in Gricignano) or on the Italian economy in the surrounding towns using OHA.

data.q: What about schools and the hospital?
data.a: DoDEA schools and U.S. Naval Hospital Naples are both at the Support Site (Gricignano), along with the commissary.

data.q: Is it a good tour for travel?
data.a: Excellent. Rome, Pompeii, the Amalfi Coast, and the rest of Europe are all within reach.
